share_basis_note: 156.54M shares outstanding x $359.60 (2026-07-10 close, last <= as_of) = ~$56.29B. Price from Yahoo chart API [S010], independently re-fetched and cross-checked vs stockanalysis (both $359.60) per freshness.json. NOTE the extreme recent volatility: $483.84 close (2026-06-30) -> $343.11 (2026-07-07), a ~-29% peak-to-trough in one week on a sector-wide memory-oversupply / AI-valuation reset (NOT company-specific news; analysts held ratings) [S011]. 52wk range $88.60-$487.91 -- the stock is a ~4x-off-low, -26%-off-high AI-test cyclical.

module_signals.finding: Anchor quarter (Q1 2026, reported 2026-04-28) and FY2025 fully primary-sourced (8-K PR + call): Q1'26 rev $1,282.5M (+87% Y/Y), Semi Test $1,111M / Product Test $80M / Robotics $91M, GAAP GM 60.9%, NI $398.9M, EPS $2.53; FY2025 rev $3,190.0M, op income $650.1M, NI $554.0M, FCF $450M. Multi-year revenue series (2021 $3.70B -> 2023 $2.68B trough -> 2025 $3.19B) sourced. Price 2-source cross-checked. Gaps (Q1'26 10-Q line items, 10-K segment op-income for 2025/2026, proxy/operator detail) are haircuts, not vetoes.

TER is the wrong tool for the batch thesis: the money is Semiconductor Test (ATE) -- 86.6% of Q1'26 revenue -- riding an AI-test supercycle (custom AI-accelerator ASICs, networking, HBM DRAM test); ~70% of company revenue is AI-tied. This is a real, primary-sourced demand surge, BUT it is a CYCLICAL semi-capital-equipment surge, not an embodied-AI/robotics story. The robotics segment (Universal Robots cobots + MiR AMRs) that made this batch look at TER is only ~7% of revenue and is loss-making. Signal +1 (real high-quality franchise) not +2 (cyclical, at peak, thesis-mismatched).

Two very different franchises. (1) Semi Test = a genuine ATE DUOPOLY (Teradyne + Advantest) with high know-how, long customer-qualification cycles and switching costs -> a real, durable oligopoly bottleneck -- but structurally CYCLICAL (peak-to-trough revenue swings of ~30%). (2) Robotics = LOW/MED barrier: cobots (Fanuc/ABB/Doosan/Techman/JAKA) and AMRs are commoditizing; UR's ease-of-use/UR+ ecosystem is a real but eroding edge, NOT a hard bottleneck; segment is sub-scale and BELOW its own ~$365M breakeven -> loss-making (Q1'25 op loss $22M). Operator: CEO Greg Smith (semi-test lifer), disciplined capital return ($785M returned FY2025 = 174% of FCF, buyback raised to up to $1B) -- provisional grade 3.5/5 pending proxy. Cyclical -> ceiling capped below Confirmed.

High-quality but cyclical financials. FY2025: rev $3,190M, GAAP GM 58.2%, op margin 20.4%, NI $554M, OCF $674.4M, capex $224.0M, FCF $450M. Balance sheet near net-cash (Dec'25 cash $293.8M + mkt sec $28.2M; ~$400M cash+investments at Q1'26; short-term debt $0, small revolver draw ~$165M for April M&A), equity ~$3.14B -- survivable through any semi downturn. BUT owner earnings are cycle-distorted: 2026 is a peak year (Q1 NI $398.9M alone ~= 72% of all of FY2025 NI), so trailing figures over-state normalized power; Q2'26 guide is DOWN sequentially and management flags 'limited visibility into 2H'. Robotics is a cash DRAG, not a contributor.

No structural breakage or balance-sheet risk (net-cash, duopoly intact) -> no hard veto. But two live trap inputs: (1) CAPITAL-CYCLE / cycle-peak: ~70% AI-tied revenue at record margins is the visible, consensus demand -- the exact 'demand>supply is already priced' setup; the 2026-07 sector selloff on HBM/memory oversupply + reports of chipmakers slowing capacity expansion is a supply-side capital-cycle signal that TER's test demand may be at/near a peak. (2) Robotics-as-concept: the embodied-AI 'option' is loss-making and stagnant (FY2024 $365M -> FY2025 ~$308M), so paying up for it as a physical-AI call is a value trap. Neither kills the thesis; both are SIZE + no-chase inputs.

At $359.60 ($56.29B) the stock is ~102x FY2025 GAAP NI ($554M) and ~40-43x even the exploding 2026E earnings, and ~65x+ a mid-cycle-normalized number -- a rich multiple for a CYCLICAL whose current print is a probable peak. Even a structural-AI BULL case (2026 run-rate becomes a durable, growing floor) barely supports today's $56B cap over 5 years; the BASE case (2026 is a cyclical peak, revenue mean-reverts) implies a negative 5y IRR from here. PRICE IS THE BINDING CONSTRAINT and it is the primary -2. No margin of safety; the batch's robotics thesis does not add value here. New money WATCH / no-chase.

capital_cycle.segment_judged: robotics (per batch mandate) + note on semi-test
capital_cycle.stage: robotics: early / trough-exiting (industrial-automation downturn 2023-25, 4 quarters of sequential recovery off a low base); semi-test (dominant): late / possible-peak
capital_cycle.supply_barrier: robotics: low-med (cobot/AMR commoditizing, many entrants); semi-test: high (ATE duopoly, know-how + qualification switching costs)
capital_cycle.read: On the ROBOTICS segment the lens says: supply CAN respond (low-med barrier), the segment is recovering from a trough but is below its own breakeven and has no durable bottleneck -> this is an option/concept, not a NVDA-style locked-in bottleneck. We do NOT know whether the robotics recovery is a real demand inflection or just industrial-automation cyclical mean-reversion. On the DOMINANT semi-test segment the lens flags late/peak: visible ~70% AI-tied demand at ATH margins is consensus, and the 2026-07 HBM-oversupply selloff + reports of capacity-expansion slowdowns are classic late-cycle supply signals.
capital_cycle.caution: PEAK (semi-test, the value driver) -> compress verdict toward WATCH / no-chase. Robotics = TROUGH-WATCHLIST only (monitor for a genuine profitable inflection; do NOT add size for it -- low barrier, loss-making, timing unknowable).

starter_zone: No new money at $359.60. A structured STARTER (~3-4%) is considerable only on a pullback toward ~$250-280, where the forward multiple on a haircut (mid-cycle, not peak-2026) earnings base compresses to ~28-32x and cyclical downside is cushioned -- AND only if the AI-test demand is still printing (no evidence the surge has rolled over).
add_zone: Scale toward Confirmed (~8-10%, cyclical ceiling) only into ~$180-220 (a genuine cycle-fear washout that does NOT break the ATE duopoly thesis) AND/OR on confirmation that the AI-test step-up is structural (multiple quarters of test demand holding + Robotics crossing breakeven into sustained profit).
trim_or_no_chase_zone: Current $359.60 is a hard NO-CHASE: ~40-43x peak-2026 earnings, ~102x FY2025, only -26% off a $487.91 ATH after a 4x run off the low. Existing holders HOLD (business unimpaired, no kill fired) but should not add; consider TRIM only if position is oversized or price re-approaches the ATH on unchanged fundamentals.
binding_constraint: PRICE + CYCLE POSITION. At $359.60 there is no margin of safety on a probable peak-cycle earnings print, and the capital-cycle lens flags the dominant semi-test demand as late/possible-peak (2026-07 memory-oversupply selloff). The robotics 'embodied-AI option' -- the reason this batch looked at TER -- is loss-making and stagnant, so it does not underwrite the price. Completeness ~58% independently caps the verdict at WATCH.
kill_criteria: Semi Test bookings/revenue roll over sequentially for 2+ quarters (AI-test demand was a pull-forward peak, not a structural step-up) -> thesis = cyclical peak confirmed.
HBM / memory-test or AI-ASIC-test oversupply materializes (customers slow test-capacity buys) -> the 2026-07 selloff fear becomes real.
Robotics stays below its ~$365M breakeven / operating losses persist or widen -> the embodied-AI option is impaired, mark it to ~zero.
Advantest or a new entrant takes structural SoC/memory-test share (duopoly erosion) -> the ONLY escalate-to-veto path on the core franchise.
Gross margin breaks below the ~55% cyclical floor, or a large inventory write-down -> earnings quality cracking.
Price stays above ~$270 with no mid-cycle-normalized earnings support -> remains WATCH / no-chase (the case at $359.60).
runner_dissent: The mechanical modules (M1 +2, M2 +1, M3 +1, M4 +1) look like they could support a STARTER, and the ATE duopoly is genuinely high-quality -- a bull could argue AI test is a structural step-change and TER is a picks-and-shovels winner. I override to WATCH for three reasons the modules under-weight: (1) M6 is a -2, not just -1 -- unlike NVDA (where price was the ONLY issue on an exceptional non-cyclical franchise), here the multiple (~40-43x PEAK-2026, ~102x FY2025) sits on a CYCLICAL earnings peak, so both price AND cycle position are against new money; (2) the batch's actual reason to look at TER -- deployed robotics revenue -- FAILS on the evidence: robotics is ~7% of revenue, loss-making, and below its own breakeven, so there is no 'robotics bottleneck already making money' here; (3) capital-cycle PEAK caution on the semi-test driver + the 2026-07 memory-oversupply selloff argue this is late-cycle, not a buy point. Completeness ~58% caps at WATCH regardless. Direction: right business quality on the ATE half, wrong price and wrong (robotics) thesis -> WATCH with a clear STARTER trigger at ~$250-280 on non-peak earnings. OPEN items left: Q1'26 10-Q balance sheet detail (O1), 2025/2026 segment operating income (O2), proxy/operator life-arc (O3), exact FY2025 robotics revenue (O4, ~$308M derived from disclosed quarters), Advantest share trend (O5).
